title: Error: error resolving storage path for container XXX lstat XXX/merged: invalid argument
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run centos:7

**Describe the results you received:**

```
#  podman run centos:7
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 24392` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 24392` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
Resolving "centos" using unqualified-search registries (/etc/containers/registries.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/centos:7...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 2d473b07cdd5 done  
Copying config 8652b9f0cb done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
Error: error resolving storage path for container 40d19a5448e6d4b678485fd877a4c6a968358b031ab1eff500fe411883979040: lstat /$HOME/.local/share/containers/storage/overlay/c76aebd2f994dfde1d36fcc3aee96e82d026261110273cd01732c2a964f34401/merged: invalid argument
```


**Describe the results you expected:**

run and shell into a container.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 24392` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
WARN[0000] The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available 
WARN[0000] For using systemd, you may need to login using an user session 
WARN[0000] Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 24392` (possibly as root) 
WARN[0000] Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs    
Version:      3.1.2
API Version:  3.1.2
Go Version:   go1.15.10
Git Commit:   51b8ddbc22cf5b10dd76dd9243924aa66ad7db39-dirty
Built:        Tue Jan  1 01:00:00 1980
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

podman was installed by ansible.

```
package podman is not installed
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes.
**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

OS: Centos 7

comments.body: Can you provide further details about the system - is /home on a network filesystem (NFS or similar)? Does running Podman as root work? Do you have any details about how Ansible is installing Podman?

I just tried this [role](https://github.com/alvistack/ansible-role-podman) mentioned in the installation instructions [here](https://podman.io/getting-started/installation).  Home is not on a network file system. I could install/run podman `2.2.1` using `yum` but with lot of post-installations modifications that could be automated by the ansible role.

Could you try with Podman 2.2.1?  I've not seen this issue until now.  I am also surprised about Podman v3.1 on a CentOS 7 system.  The kubic packages were supposed to stop at 3.0.X.

@lsm5 did we update beyond 3.0 for 7?
I don't think this is solvable without further details on how the Ansible script works - where is it getting its Podman, how is it installing it, etc.

> Could you try with Podman 2.2.1? I've not seen this issue until now. I am also surprised about Podman v3.1 on a CentOS 7 system. The kubic packages were supposed to stop at 3.0.X.
> 
> @lsm5 did we update beyond 3.0 for 7?

I confirm, the script worked on CentOS 8 and it installed version `2.2.1`

> I don't think this is solvable without further details on how the Ansible script works - where is it getting its Podman, how is it installing it, etc.

as I mentioned, the ansible script comes from the official podman documentation, it's not mine.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

@statiksof, apologies for the long silence.

I fear that CentOS 7 is too old to run Podman 3.X. CentOS 8 will work.
